preview_text:
The model assigns Uruguay a 74% chance of victory, with Cape Verde's win probability at just 5%. That 69-percentage-point gap reflects a substantial disparity in the forecast; the underdog's path to three points is narrow on the model's assessment.

The draw emerges as the secondary outcome in the model view, at 21%, suggesting meaningful competitive balance exists despite Uruguay's heavy favoritism. The win probabilities skew sharply toward the favorite, but the inclusion of a one-in-five draw chance indicates the match is not modeled as a foregone conclusion—Cape Verde retains realistic scope to avoid defeat.

The sportsbook consensus leans less heavily toward Uruguay, pricing the favorite at 64% versus the model's 74%. Bookmakers assign Cape Verde roughly 13% win probability, compared to the model's 5%—a gap of 8 percentage points. Polymarket aligns closer to the books. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these readings; the divergence suggests material disagreement on the underdog's ceiling.
